intelligence_gaps.gap: High-confidence tier (conf 70-84) is producing 28.6% win rate across 7 bets — worse than medium confidence — suggesting the confidence calibration model is systematically overconfident on certain signal combinations.
intelligence_gaps.why: If high-confidence picks are winning at 28.6% vs 53.8% for medium, the confidence scoring is inversely correlated with outcomes at the top tier, which means staking more on 'high' picks is destroying value faster than medium picks.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: After 10+ more settled high-confidence bets, consider whether the high-confidence threshold should require BOTH NetRtg gap AND injury confirmation (not just one), essentially requiring two independent positive signals before assigning conf ≥ 70.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Only 7 high-confidence bets settled — below the 20-bet threshold required for confidence_staking patches per rules; need more sample size before restructuring tiers.

intelligence_gaps.gap: Spread market is -€319.06 on 8 bets (3W/5L, 37.5%) — far underperforming ML (45.5%) — but the current rules don't distinguish which spread signal types are failing (B2B, NetRtg gap, tanking opponent, or DefRtg gap).
intelligence_gaps.why: Without knowing which spread signal drove the 5 losses, the market_rules spread section cannot be selectively tightened — we risk either over-restricting valid signals or leaving the bad ones untouched.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Tag each settled spread bet with its primary signal type (B2B, NetRtg gap, tank-opponent, DefRtg) in the bet log so Analyst can identify which sub-signal has the worst record and tighten that specific rule.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Infrastructure/logging fix needed in Settler output — cannot produce a targeted rule patch without signal-level breakdown of spread losses.

intelligence_gaps.gap: Odds range 2.10-2.50 is 0W/3L (0%, -€229.31) — every bet placed at these odds has lost — but the current odds_targets ceiling of 2.50 does not penalise or restrict picks in this range.
intelligence_gaps.why: Three losses from three bets at 2.10-2.50 suggests either the picks in this range are being selected with insufficient edge, or the market is efficient at these odds and the system's confidence estimates are uncalibrated at higher odds.
intelligence_gaps.suggestion: Consider temporarily lowering the ML ceiling to 2.10 until the 2.10-2.50 range accumulates a sample of 8+ bets, OR add a rule requiring EV ≥ 0.10 (instead of 0.05) for picks with odds ≥ 2.10 as a higher evidence bar.
intelligence_gaps.not_patched_reason: Only 3 bets in this range — below threshold for strategic section changes (need 10+ per ev_requirement rules); flagging for monitoring but confidence is 0.60, just below the 0.70 patch threshold.
